title: Subsets II
problem_statement: Given an integer array nums that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets (the power set) without duplicate subsets.

examples.explanation: Subsets are unique despite duplicate values.

intuition: Sort the array and use backtracking, skipping duplicate values at the same recursion depth.

approaches.name: Backtrack with Duplicate Skip
approaches.description: Sort nums, then build subsets recursively while skipping identical values that would start duplicate branches.

interview_qa.a: Sorting groups duplicates so we can easily skip repeating branches.
Yes, the empty set is always included in the power set.
